pi_items.verified_value: Synthetic data embedded directly in setup_task.sh shell script as XML
pi_items.source: Code analysis: setup_task.sh lines 25-107 contain hardcoded XML with 60 observations of synthetic data. The dataset is generated from this embedded data during task setup, not downloaded or referenced from any real CPS source.

pi_items.metadata_value: Real data from Census Bureau Current Population Survey
pi_items.source: Direct inspection of setup_task.sh: the entire 60-observation dataset with all variable values is hardcoded in the bash script as embedded XML. No download of real data occurs. The XML description mentions CPS as a reference context, but the actual data values are fabricated.

privileged_info_summary: The dataset contains 60 synthetic observations of 8 variables (wage, educ, exper, female, black, metro, south, west). The task requires creating derived variables lwage (natural log of wage) and exper2 (experience squared), then running an OLS regression with 8 regressors plus constant. All file paths are correctly specified: script at /home/ga/Documents/gretl_output/vif_analysis.inp, model output at wage_model.txt, and VIF results at vif_results.txt. The data is entirely synthetic, generated from hardcoded XML embedded in the setup script, not derived from real Census Bureau CPS data despite the task description's claim.
